title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple and Lucy Ashebrook discuss the family's varying reactions to the matter of Matilda. Lucy reports that Harold and Alfred believe Matilda is a hoax, while Cedric views the situation as non-serious extortion. Brian believes the matter is authentic, though Lucy notes that Brian is easily fooled by appearances. Inspector Claddock warns Lucy that the men paying her attention may intend to harm her rather than woo her. Miss Marple expresses concern for the children, specifically Alexander, fearing that their tendency to search for clues might put them in danger.

Regarding the murder, Miss Marple notes that the killer was described as a tall, dark-haired man, and she observes that there are three such brothers at Larchfield Manor who appeared identical in thick coats during the inquest. The group discusses a potential motive involving Matilda: if her story is true, her reappearance with a son could divert the Crickenshaw inheritance. It is revealed that Harold, despite his apparent wealth, needs money to avoid bankruptcy due to poor investments. Lucy adds that the old Mr. Crickenshaw previously suggested his doctor might have been exaggerating his illness to make it appear as though he had been poisoned.

Later, Claddock meets with Dr. Campbell. The doctor, frustrated by a difficult operation, discusses a woman who delayed medical treatment. When Claddock thanks him for suggesting Emma bring the letter regarding the late brother's widow, Dr. Campbell clarifies that Emma initiated the idea. He expresses doubt that the letter was actually written by Matilda, suggesting instead that someone is exploiting Emma's feelings to take advantage of the situation.
